seo_title: Did AI Assist Pope Leo XIV in Writing About AI's Dangers?
meta_description: An analysis by Linch Zhang found that parts of Pope Leo XIV's encyclical about AI's impact on humanity may have been written by AI.
body_html:
It is possible that AI was used to write parts of Pope Leo XIV's latest encyclical about AI's impact on humanity.

AI Detection Methods UsedPangram, a popular AI detector, found certain paragraphs to be between 40% and 100% written by AI.The document includes known traits that appear in AI-generated writing, such as a higher use of the word “genuinely”.A 62% confidence rating was found for the first chapter of the document when run through Pangram.Estimated AI ContentSectionEstimated AI ContentFirst Chapter62%Roughly 2,000 words of the document46%Limitations of AI DetectionPangram is not foolproof and different detectors can display different results.

source.title: Did the Pope use AI to write about the dangers of AI?
source.summary: Pope Leo XIV's latest encyclical, "Magnifica Humanitas", may contain parts written by artificial intelligence (AI). An analysis found that certain paragraphs in the document were likely generated by AI, with a detection tool estimating that they were between 40% and 100% written by machine. The use of AI in writing the encyclical has sparked debate about its implications for human authorship and creativity.
